label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's think about this step-by-step. 1. I start at a point, let's call it point A. 2. I take 3 steps forward in some direction, say north. I'm now at point B, which is 3 units north of A. 3. I take 7 more steps in the same direction (north), so I'm at point C, which is 10 units north of A. 4. I turn around, so I'm now facing south. 5. I take 10 steps in the south direction. Since I was 10 units north of A, and I take 10 steps south, I end up right back at point A. So yes, if you follow these instructions, you return to the starting point. The total steps in the
